service_line_config.name: salesforce
service_line_config.tech_context: This project is built on Salesforce platform. Key technologies and concepts: - Core Platform: Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud, Marketing Cloud - UI Development: Lightning Web Components (LWC), Aura Components, Lightning App Builder - Backend: Apex classes, triggers, batch jobs, scheduled jobs, queueable jobs - Data: SOQL, SOSL, standard/custom objects, relationships (lookup, master-detail, junction) - Automation: Flows (record-triggered, screen, scheduled), Process Builder, Approval Processes - Security: Profiles, Permission Sets, Sharing Rules, Field-Level Security, org-wide defaults - Integration: REST/SOAP/Bulk/Streaming APIs, Platform Events, Change Data Capture - Middleware: MuleSoft, Boomi, Informatica for external system integration - DevOps: Salesforce DX, scratch orgs, change sets, CI/CD pipelines - Governance: Governor Limits (API callouts, SOQL queries, DML operations, heap size, CPU time) - Environments: Production, sandboxes (Developer, Partial, Full), scratch orgs

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to Salesforce functional areas where applicable: - Portal/community features → Experience Cloud - CRM features → Sales Cloud or Service Cloud capabilities - Data and object model areas → Salesforce Object model - Integration features → middleware and API patterns - Security and access control → Salesforce security model Do NOT create modules for standard Salesforce administration — focus on what is being built or customized.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: Reference Salesforce platform capabilities in requirements: - Specify whether a requirement uses declarative features (clicks) or custom development (code) - Reference Salesforce objects (standard or custom) when relevant - For portal requirements, reference Experience Cloud components and capabilities - For integration requirements, specify the API type and middleware - Note Governor Limit implications for data-intensive or bulk requirements
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: Include Salesforce-specific non-functional requirements: - Governor Limits compliance: API callout limits, SOQL query limits, DML limits per transaction - Data storage and file storage limits - Platform availability (trust.salesforce.com SLA dependencies) - Large data volume (LDV) considerations: indexing, skinny tables, archival strategy - Login and session security policies - Sharing model performance for role hierarchy and sharing rules - Sandbox refresh cadence and test data strategy
artifact_overrides.task: Include Salesforce-specific implementation tasks where applicable: - Object model: custom objects, fields, relationships, page layouts, record types - UI: Lightning pages, LWC components, Experience Cloud configuration - Backend: Apex classes, triggers, batch/scheduled/queueable jobs - Automation: Flows, approval processes, validation rules - Integration: Named Credentials, External Services, middleware configuration, API development - Security: profiles, permission sets, sharing rules, field-level security setup - Data: migration scripts, data loader configuration, transformation rules - Deployment: change sets, Salesforce DX packages, sandbox promotion
artifact_overrides.test_case: Include Salesforce-specific testing considerations: - Test with multiple user profiles and permission sets to verify access control - Test bulk operations against Governor Limit thresholds - Test portal/community access for both authenticated and guest users where applicable - Test integrations with mock responses, error handling, and retry scenarios - Verify sharing rule behavior across different roles and territories - Apex test classes must meet minimum 75% code coverage requirement
artifact_overrides.architecture: Describe architecture using Salesforce platform terminology: - Org strategy: single-org vs multi-org, sandbox topology - Object model: standard vs custom objects, relationship types, record types - UI architecture: Lightning App Builder pages, custom LWC components, Experience Cloud templates - Integration architecture: API patterns, middleware layer, event-driven vs request-response - Security architecture: org-wide defaults, role hierarchy, sharing model, encryption - Automation architecture: declarative (Flows) vs programmatic (Apex) decision criteria - Deployment architecture: environments, promotion path, CI/CD approach Do NOT use generic web architecture terms when Salesforce-specific terms exist.
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: Include Salesforce platform-specific risks: - Governor Limit violations under high data volumes or complex transactions - Salesforce seasonal release impact (3 major releases per year may affect custom code) - License costs and limits (API calls, data storage, feature licenses) - Middleware reliability and external API rate limits - Data migration complexity and volume constraints - Over-customization leading to technical debt (custom code vs declarative solutions) - Sandbox limitations affecting testing quality
